question_title: Will Apple publish an iOS or iPadOS security update noting active exploitation before May 1, 2026?
summary: Apple publishes iOS/iPadOS security updates noting active exploitation at a rate of approximately 8-12+ per year. In a 47-day window (March 15 - May 1, 2026), even conservative Poisson estimates give 64-79% probability. This is pushed much higher by: (1) CISA adding 3 iOS vulnerabilities to its KEV catalog on March 5, 2026 — just 10 days before the window opens — confirming active exploitation Apple must address; (2) Apple already patching an iOS zero-day (CVE-2026-20700) in February 2026 showing elevated threat activity; (3) the broad resolution definition counting "may have been actively exploited" language; and (4) the continuous pipeline of zero-day discoveries from the commercial spyware ecosystem. The only NO scenario requires an unprecedented 47-day pause in iOS active exploitation disclosures during a period of elevated threat activity.

factors.description: Historical base rate: Apple discloses ~8-12 actively exploited iOS/iPadOS vulnerabilities per year, giving 64-79% probability of at least one in a 47-day window via Poisson model
CISA added 3 iOS vulnerabilities to KEV catalog on March 5, 2026 (just 10 days before window opens), indicating active exploitation that Apple needs to address with patches
Apple already patched CVE-2026-20700 (iOS zero-day) in February 2026, showing elevated threat activity in early 2026
Broad resolution definition includes 'may have been actively exploited' language, capturing suspected exploitation reports as well
Commercial spyware ecosystem (NSO Group etc.) and nation-state actors continuously discover iOS vulnerabilities - structural driver ensures steady supply of zero-days
No known 47-day gap without at least one such iOS/iPadOS disclosure in the past 2-3 years based on historical pattern

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.97 (97% YES)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **Base rate is very high**: Apple discloses ~8-12+ actively exploited iOS/iPadOS vulnerabilities per year. A Poisson model gives 64-79% probability of at least one in the 47-day window even at conservative rates.
- **CISA just flagged 3 iOS vulns**: On March 5, 2026 (10 days before the window opens), CISA added 3 iOS vulnerabilities to its KEV catalog, confirming active exploitation. Apple will need to address these.
- **2026 is already active**: Apple patched CVE-2026-20700 (dyld zero-day) in February 2026, discovered by Google TAG — indicating nation-state/spyware activity.
- **Broad definition helps YES**: The resolution counts "may have been actively exploited" language, not just confirmed exploitation.
- **Structural drivers persist**: The commercial spyware industry ensures a steady pipeline of iOS zero-day discoveries.

The only NO scenario would require an unprecedented ~7-week pause in iOS active exploitation disclosures during a period of elevated threat activity, which has no precedent in recent years.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Apple publish an iOS or iPadOS security update noting active exploitation before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42515)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if Apple publishes an iOS or iPadOS security release note on or after March 15, 2026 and before May 1, 2026 that includes language indicating the vulnerability was actively exploited (for example, “actively exploited” or equivalent). This question resolves to no if no such iOS or iPadOS security release note is published in that period. The primary source is Apple’s Security Updates pages at [https://support.apple.com/en-us/100100](https://support.apple.com/en-us/100100)
fine_print:
Count only iOS or iPadOS updates (not macOS, Safari, watchOS, or third-party apps). Use the publication date shown on the Apple security note page. If wording disputes arise, resolve using credible sources per [https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitionsCandidate](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitionsCandidate) Sources: Apple Security Updates index (backup) [https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201222](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201222) | Apple iOS/iPadOS security release note pages linked from the index (backup)

Please note that reports of suspected exploitation counts for resolution. For example, Apple's 17.7.6 [update](https://support.apple.com/en-us/122372) saying, "Apple is aware of a report that this issue may have been actively exploited" would count.
